items.title: Locals fear for jobs as Port Pirie smelter's future in doubt

children.content: Locals say the possible closure of the Port Pirie smelter and the loss of the 900 jobs associated with it would have ongoing effects on the city and the wider Spencer Gulf region.

articles.title: Smelter jobs at stake as government assistance runs dry

children.content: South Australia's premier says there is a "national imperative" to keep smelters running, as a $135 million assistance package for Nyrstar operations at Port Pirie and Hobart expires.

image.altText: An aerial view of the Nyrstar smelter in Port Pirie

gallery.altText: The sign for Ellen Street in the foreground with a large smoke stack from the lead smelter in the background

thumbnail.altText: The sign for Ellen Street in the foreground with a large smoke stack from the lead smelter in the background

items.title: Police confirm identity of remains found in SA bushland

children.content: SA Police say remains found in bushland south of Adelaide a week ago have been identified as belonging to Jeff Mundy, who was last seen by his family five years ago.

image.altText: Two women and a man stand near a fence with a bouquet of flowers attached to it, with trees behind

items.title: 'Children have ended up on main roads', SA childcare regulator says

children.content: South Australia’s childcare centre watchdog says children have injured themselves or "ended up on main roads" due to poor supervision and that one in five services in the state do not meet national quality standards.

image.altText: A man in a dark blue suit and tie is sitting and looking ahead

items.title: SA government needs certainty on LIV Golf by October, premier says

children.content: The SA Premier says the government is "monitoring" the situation regarding LIV Golf closely, and would need a guarantee of its future in Adelaide by October.

articles.title: LIV Golf is falling apart on a flawed premise and a broken promise

children.content: While Adelaide showed just how good LIV Golf could be, the flawed premise of the breakaway tournament always spelled trouble.

image.altText: GettyImages-2147098984

articles.title: Aussie one of several big names left in the lurch as LIV Golf funding pulled

children.content: Australia's Cameron Smith is one of several big names in golf left with an uncertain future after Saudi Arabia pulled funding of LIV Golf.

image.altText: A golfer tees off.

gallery.altText: Three men smiling and walking, two of them in sportswear and one in a business suit

thumbnail.altText: Three men smiling and walking, two of them in sportswear and one in a business suit

items.title: Remembering SA's pain and battles behind the Murray-Darling Basin Plan

children.content: The Murray-Darling Basin Plan is under review, but with submissions due to close today, we take a step back in time to remember when dried lake beds were turning acidic, dairy farmers were selling up, and there was talk of water carting for Adelaide.

image.altText: Close up of cracks in dried lakebed

items.title: The algal bloom may be 'gone', but many are still grappling with the fall-out

children.content: While testing has shown a dramatic improvement in South Australia's algal bloom situation, industry and experts fear it won't be a quick recovery.

image.altText: A mural of a squid painted on the wall of a tackle shop.

items.title: Police charge man for allegedly attending businesses while nude

children.content: A Mount Gambier man is granted bail after he was allegedly nude while in businesses and on a busy main road three times in the past week.

image.altText: A police officer in a cap standing in front of a police station

items.title: Adelaide skipper ruled out of Showdown after brother's funeral

children.content: Adelaide Crow's skipper Jordan Dawson has been ruled out of Friday's Showdown following his brother's sudden death.

image.altText: Jordan Dawson shares a heartfelt hug with Hugh McCluggage

items.title: ATSB confirms student and instructor killed in Parafield plane crash

children.content: The ATSB said the plane had been doing circuit training at the airport, but it's not yet known if structured training was underway when it crashed.

image.altText: Black smoke billows from a plane crash at Parafield Airport

items.title: Electrifying your home comes with savings, but conversion costs stopping many

children.content: A new report says the money consumers could save by converting from gas to electricity is set to increase over the coming decades, sparking calls for government regulation to encourage the switch.

image.altText: A yellow sign on a wooden post which reads 'we're electrifying!'

items.title: Long wait for SA upper house result creating 'confusion and uncertainty'

children.content: With less than a week until state parliament resumes, an incoming upper house member says she still cannot access the building or appoint staff because the election result is yet to be declared.

image.altText: A woman with brown hair wearing a blue dress

items.title: Daughter continues dad's Murray fight as 'transformational moment' looms

children.content: Julie Jones has lived by the River Murray all her life and wants all South Australians to participate in the first comprehensive review of the Murray-Darling Basin Plan.

image.altText: A woman on a deck with a river in the background

items.title: Poliovirus detected in Perth wastewater

children.content: Poliovirus has been detected in a sample taken from a wastewater in Perth, the Health Department reveals.

image.altText: am illustration of a polio virus particle against a black background

items.title: Hard-won legacy of Australia's longest strike in jeopardy

children.content: It's been 80 years since Aboriginal workers in the Pilbara up-ended a slave-like labour system. The descendants of those involved fear its legacy is being forgotten.

gallery.altText: The eyes of an elderly Aboriginal man with white hair.
A wide angle shot of an older white man sitting alone in a large shed.
A trio of young Aboriginal boys climb out of a public pool.

thumbnail.altText: The eyes of an elderly Aboriginal man with white hair.
A wide angle shot of an older white man sitting alone in a large shed.
A trio of young Aboriginal boys climb out of a public pool.

items.title: Melbourne and Sydney record large house price falls as growth slows across the country

children.content: Momentum in the housing market has slowed, with every capital city recording a slower pace of growth in April.

image.altText: A new housing estate stretches as far as the eye can see.

items.title: 'Gentle parenting' under fire from this alternative school's supporters

children.content: For 50 years, this alternative school sold parents a vision of progressive education. But some who have passed through its doors say a 'cult-like' culture enabled decades of unchecked physical abuse.

image.altText: A man in a school classroom surrounded by students whose faces are obscured

gallery.altText: A number of kids sit at a round table. Their identities have been hidden.

thumbnail.altText: A number of kids sit at a round table. Their identities have been hidden.

items.title: Australian 'ISIS brides' in limbo after reportedly being blocked from reaching Syrian airport

children.content: A week since a group of 13 Australian women and children with ties to former Islamic State fighters left a Syrian refugee camp for Australia, they remain in the capital Damascus in a state of limbo.

image.altText: A young girl and three women walking towards a mini bus.

items.title: The battery rebate became a victim of its success. Here's what is changing

children.content: In less than nine months, more than a quarter of a million batteries were installed in Australian households. Here's how the battery rebate is changing and why.

image.altText: Leanne Phillips stands next to a work truck in a driveway in Toowoomba, April 2026.

items.title: ASX rebounds after seven losing sessions led by miners, ANZ shares slump despite profit jump — as it happened

children.content: The ASX rebounds after seven losing sessions but ANZ's profit announcement drags down the banks.

image.altText: A stock trader looks down at a tablet as he works on the floor of a busy stock exchange while surrounded by screens.

items.title: Is the new Foo Fighters the best album of April?

children.content: Come for Dave Grohl and co's latest. Stay for club-facing collabs, lush disco, homegrown releases and a pair of Québécois aliens making left-field instrumental jams.

items.title: Weekly news quiz: A Trump shooting scare, a burger blue and a rescued roo

children.content: It's time to sit back after a big week in news and test how much you remember.

image.altText: Secret service agents respond during the incident at the White House correspondents' dinner.

items.title: Australian screen industry as 'intergenerational' as housing

children.content: Now in its 40th year, the annual Screen Forever conference attracts hundreds of industry leaders to look at the past, present and future of Australian screen content.

image.altText: A man stands behind a podium and in front of a projector

items.title: Banksy behind overnight reveal of new statue in central London

children.content: Elusive street artist Banksy has confirmed he is behind a new work drawing crowds in central London: a statue of a suited man striding into the void while holding a flag that blocks his view.

gallery.altText: A statue of a man carrying a flag, blinded by it, about to walk off his statue plinth.

thumbnail.altText: A statue of a man carrying a flag, blinded by it, about to walk off his statue plinth.

items.title: From near-misses and cancer to Olympic success, Glaetzer bows out

children.content: Matt Glaetzer was, for so long, the nearly man of cycling, heartbreakingly missing out on an elusive Olympic medal despite reaching five finals. But the South Australian refused to be beaten, either by that, or by cancer.

image.altText: Matt Glaetzer smiles and clenches his fist

items.title: Tense moment at FIFA Congress as delegate refuses 'absurd' handshake request

children.content: FIFA boss Gianni Infantino is criticised as he attempts to encourage Israeli and Palestinian delegates to shake hands.

items.title: Coaches and fans grumble as technology scrutiny rises

children.content: Nobody wants to see their team robbed by a missed officiating decision. But as scrutiny over video assistant refereeing or umpiring continues, some are wondering whether it's all worth it.

image.altText: Matt Stevic, and Joel Jeffrey point in opposite directions

items.title: Footage of Daniels's NBA fight sparks criticism of Aussie star

children.content: There is little support for the Hawks' Australian star Dyson Daniels after he is ejected from Game 6 of their NBA play-off game against the Knicks.

image.altText: New York Knicks center Mitchell Robinson and Atlanta Hawks guard Dyson Daniels scuffle during an NBA game.

items.title: How a future Bronco hero Jesse Southwell broke Queensland's heart

children.content: In her final act as a Novocastrian before heading to Brisbane, Jesse Southwell kicked the first field goal of her life to deliver victory for New South Wales in Origin I.

gallery.altText: Jesse Southwell reacts after kicking a field goal

thumbnail.altText: Jesse Southwell reacts after kicking a field goal

items.title: Man who stabbed father to death with kitchen knife not guilty due to insanity

children.content: A Perth man who fatally stabbed his father has been found not guilty of murder on the grounds of insanity.

image.altText: A suburban street with multiple police vehicles and police tape.

items.title: Fatal crash on remote gravel road prompts call to limit trucks

children.content: The death of a beloved school teacher in a crash with a road train has prompted a call to review the safety of an unsealed road in remote WA.

image.altText: A man with glasses and a beard takes selfie with arm around a woman with long blonde hair on a beach, both smiling

items.title: 'Neglected' seat of Farrer is turning against major parties

children.content: A fascinating four-cornered contest is brewing as voters in the southern NSW seat of Farrer head to the ballot box for the second time in 12 months.

image.altText: Political signs lined up in a row outside of a polling station.

items.title: Tradesman who helped police officer friend steal $100k from paedophile jailed

children.content: A Wollongong man drove to Lightning Ridge with his police officer friend to rob an elderly convicted paedophile of $100,000.

image.altText: Man in grey suit looks at camera while walking down stairs

items.title: Queensland mining company fined $7m after death of 'beloved Poppy'

children.content: Graham Dawson, 62, was killed in a roof collapse on a central Queensnland mining site in 2021. The District Court in Brisbane heard Mastermyne Crinum Operations was criminally negligent and the death was avoidable.

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing a Rabbitohs jersey, sitting next to his grandkids in a grandstand

items.title: UK terrorism threat level rises, man arrested after Jewish men stabbed

children.content: Britain raises its national terrorism threat level to "severe" from "substantial", a day after an antisemitic stabbing attack in north London, as Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer vows stronger action to protect Jewish people.

image.altText: A member of the forensic team is kneeling with one knee on the ground. In his hand is a plastic bag, behind him is a red car.

items.title: NT paramedics 'beaten' to the point of 'semi-consciousness'

children.content: A woman was left to suffer a seizure for two hours due to ambulance services being overwhelmed by call-outs earlier this month. The organisation's new chief executive has said unless more resources are funded to ease demand, the service could collapse.

image.altText: An ambulance pulled side of road at dusk in city
